With a heart full of gratitude in uncertain times, I declare a new battle's eve upon us. Few moons have passed since our last stand against the fleshstalkers. While our bravery and sacrifice returned victory, life is never a cost worth paying unless there is no other choice. Yet here we find ourselves in that position once more.You—in the face of uncertainty—have chosen the selfless act of volunteering to protect the lives of lowlanders, among countless others. And so it is my great honor to invite you and those dear to you to feast amongst fellow bearkin in Harvest Den, in this first quarter moon of the lunar cycle.You honor our tribe through sacrifice. On this eve, may we do the service of honoring you.Your humbled Claw,—Stoic Alder

With a renewed sense of the value of my craft, I fear my days in Harvest Den are numbered... A shame, truly. This place calms my tired spirit.    I've written a number of bespoke pieces during my time here, but I do worry about bringing too much attention to this place... My fame is unrelenting, and my pages will surely inspire others to come experience it for themselves. It was a hard choice to leave these pages behind, but I will be leaving these pieces as a gift to the lowlanders. A lingering kiss, long after a lover's farewell.    Perhaps they'll think of me as they read them.    —Snargle Goldclaw

With the formation of the alliance, the world's economy is doing quite a bit better than it has in recent years, despite the continued crises that we face. The various rebuilding efforts are strong, and charitable organizations are seeing continued support from all strata of society.    There have been a few issues with integrating Cantha into global trade networks, as some citizens of New Kaineng are so ridiculously wealthy compared to the average Krytan that it's led to a surge of so-called "tourist pricing."    For example, we interviewed an Awakened merchant on the streets of Amnoon who claimed they were just back from Arborstone. However, reports from Arborstone claimed that same merchant disappeared months ago. Either way, we noticed that they were selling Joko statues at ten times the normal cost. When we inquired about the price increase, they refused to give us a straight answer about the price or their recent travel history.
With the legions cohort is Kaeli Foestalker. She has some intel about local Inquest caches we can raid for supplies.

With the new techniques developed by Zinn, however, the days of extracting chak energy by getting elbow deep in a chitinous corpse are long gone! The new extractors are easy, safe, and painless to use. They're even mostly painless for the chak themselves!Once the chak have gathered enough ley-line energy, simply place the collectors near them and activate. The patent-pending gradient-differential system pulls the concentrated ley-line energy out from their bodies and stores it in convenient batteries. The creatures can then be allowed to return to their burrows to gather up more while you use their energy to power anything you can dream of!

Working in collaboration with an illuminate from the Astral Ward, we've begun research on the magically dense mirrors that are strewn throughout the city ruins. Upon inspection, this illuminate has informed me that these mirrors are tethered together via a type of magic called shadowcraft. Prior records on shadowcraft are brief and limited to observations of mursaat cloaking abilities.    In practice, the mirrors are portals, or gateways, and are connected to a small dimension that exists between Tyria and the Mists. When someone travels through one of these mirrors, their essence is temporarily distorted, and they are able to quickly jump from one mirror to another.

Working Title: Joko Rising (...Too on the nose, or just right?)I. Foul Fall foretells Winter; Winter worries into Spring;Spring storms bring summer swelter; and the sweltiest summer brought Joko the King II. No mother could have birthed Him; from no father did he spawn.Joko the Singular simply sprang to being, as Tyria purred along. III. Joko ended nothingness; Joko started time.And now Joko, who is bored, sends both meter and rhyme to be tortured and executed. IV. I, Joko, the Eternal Monarch, Scourge of Vabbi, the Beloved,Most lauded poet, capturing life and torturing it and then capturing its death V. And torturing that too; Poet-King Joko, who has the screams of the tormentedRinging in his ears daily, for it is Joko who torments, VI. Torments and is tormented by a lack of good help(Please apply within), hereby inscribes his name VII. And a fraction of his honorable titles, to begin, at last,His magnum opus, his masterpiece, an exquisite confection—this poem. VIII. The most noble Joko, born beneath a stormy sky.A student of sand and strife, willed into being by sweet suffering,IX. Had no mother. No father. The cries for a god were answered,And the answer was Joko. X. Fully formed, fully realized, he sprang forth,And those who saw greatness served him. XI. By Joko, this is already tedious. Must I really do this for another hundred stanzas?My time is eternal, and still I feel like I'm wasting it.

Writing this down in case something happens to me.    Skulli got word about these ruins outside this old abandoned city. No one had reported it before, even though we sailed through these parts once or twice. I don't remember the name now—Skulli was always the smart one. The important thing is...he says there's untold riches there. Just waiting for someone to discover it. Pirates always get excited over ruins.    First, I told him he's full of it, but he was insistent. And he kept going on and on...something about the look in his eye told me he weren't lying. So I gathered the rest of the crew. We made it down here, but I'm all that's left.    There's all these crazy-looking critters roaming about, looking infected or something! One of those took out Clance. And the spiders...ugh! They scared off Ajai, the coward. Don't know where she went.    We found an abandoned camp—probably not abandoned for all that long. Murph said that the clothes looked like White Mantle. Not my business to judge.    Me and Murph, we tried to get out, but something kept pushing us back in. We found some sorta sealed door that looked like it was tied to some weird glowing statues nearby. Some kinda puzzle? Murph went off to look for clues, but he's been gone a long time. Got a bad feeling...    I keep hearing rustling from above, like something's up in th—
Written by Alarrin of the Frost
Written by Loremaster Ermenred of Ascalon
